explanation:
The requirement says: 'The system shall not permit the healthcare professional operator to erase the contents of the alarm system log.' To formalize this: 

- 'the healthcare professional operator' corresponds to the atomic proposition user_equalTo_operator (i.e., user is operator).
- 'erase the contents of the alarm system log' corresponds to the atomic proposition eraseLog.
- 'shall not permit' means that, whenever the user is a healthcare professional operator, they must not erase the log. In temporal logic, this is safety: we want it to never be the case that the operator erases the log. That is, globally (at all times), if user_equalTo_operator is true, then eraseLog must never happen at that time (and because we check at every moment, at every moment eraseLog must not be true when user_equalTo_operator is true).
- The LTL implication is: Globally (user_equalTo_operator -> !eraseLog).

Thus, the LTL formula is: G(user_equalTo_operator -> !eraseLog).
